attacking_style: Launches long-range solo attacks 40km+ from finish, particularly on descents and technical sections where he can leverage superior bike handling. Also attacks early on climbs to force pacing or target intermediate sprints for bonus seconds.
team_tactics: Dual-role flexibility: either sacrificial domestique setting tempo to fracture groups for leaders, or given complete freedom for breakaway stage hunting when team GC secure. UAE grants more autonomy than Movistar did.
terrain_preference: Technical descents and short punchy climbs where superior bike handling and bridging ability shine. Vulnerable on false-flat sections and long sustained climbs where pure watts matter. ITT limitations hurt GC ambitions.
vulnerability: Exposed on false-flat and long sustained climbs when lacking pure power. Cannot match top-tier GC riders in time trials. Shows extreme form variance—brilliant or dropped, rarely consistent mid-pack.
key_patterns: Solo bridging specialist: repeatedly closes significant gaps alone over categorized climbs (51s, 1:46 deficits closed)
Descent attacker: multiple wins/attacks from technical descents, wet or dry, gaining 26-38s gaps
Long-range attacks: proven 40km+ solo efforts, including Paris-Nice GC winning 40km attack
Bonus second hunter: targets intermediate sprints from breakaways when in GC contention
Form volatility: crashes/abandons contrast with stage wins, inconsistent week-to-week performance
Team freedom indicator: unauthorized breaks and solo moves signal leadership confidence or tactical irrelevance
form_trajectory: neutral: 2023 Vuelta showed strong climbing support for UAE but GC aspirations faded mid-race. Mixed signals between tactical contributions and personal ambition suggest stable domestique role.
